endpoints.name: Solrock / Lunatone pair (precedent invoked + caveat invoked)

description: Wave 45 E3 considered TWO candidate moves on the Gen I counterpart-pair axis and refused BOTH. Move 1: `magmar-electabuzz-pair` solo-pair character node modeled on the solrock-lunatone-pair (Hoenn version-exclusive Meteorite-Pokemon dyad founded at N=3 corpus cards across 2 species, wave 23). Current Magmar/Electabuzz state: N=2 corpus cards (RBC-029 + RBC-058), 2 species, same set, designer-coordinated production pairing — but N=2 is the WAVE 18 deferral state of solrock-lunatone-pair, NOT the founding state (N=3). Wave 32-33 trivia + Edgelord already mirrored the 1:1 Connections edge between Magmar and Electabuzz on canonical pair grounds; the 1:1 mirror is sufficient at N=2. Move 2: `gen1-counterpart-pair` generic symbol or hub node unifying multiple Gen I version-exclusive pairs (Magmar/Electabuzz, Vulpix/Growlithe, etc.). REFUSED per the solrock-lunatone-pair wave-23 caveat: 'other sun/moon Pokemon pairs would warrant their own character nodes if they enter the corpus, NOT a generic Pokemon sun/moon duality hub. Membership predicates on the species identity AND the paired-design thesis together.' The established graph convention is instance-level per-pair character nodes, NOT a pattern-level wrapper. Both moves filed to triage with the per-pair threshold trigger and the no-generic-wrapper architectural decision documented. The graph's existing convention is sound; Edgelord declines to override it.
citations: [Bulbapedia: Magmar; Bulbapedia: Electabuzz; List of Pokemon with counterparts]
